text: Day 48 PM roundup: Trump announces historic Netanyahu-Aoun call (1st in 34 yrs). Pakistan 'major breakthrough' on Iran nuclear — Munir-Araghchi meeting in Tehran. IDF kills Hezbollah coastal cmdr Baz + LRB cmdr Hamdan. Hezbollah drones hit 146th Division base Nahariya + Liman. 5 IDF wounded Bint Jbeil. IDF Chief Zamir heightened readiness; IAF preparing Iran strike packages. Netanyahu: 5 divisions in Lebanon buffer zone; enrichment 'annulled.' Blockade Day 3: 10 ships turned back. S&P 7,033 (record). Oil: Brent $94.89 WTI $91.50. Gas: $4.09 (down 2¢). Sanctions waiver April 19.
verificationNote: Compiled from Al Jazeera, CNBC, Iran International, JPost, Israel Hayom, Tribune India, Tempo.co, Trading Economics, PBS, AAA.

text: BREAKING: Trump announces 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire starting 5 PM ET (21:00 GMT). 'First meaningful talks since 1983.' Aoun and Netanyahu invited to White House. CRITICAL CAVEAT: Israel says IDF will NOT withdraw from southern Lebanon — will maintain 'freedom of action' against Hezbollah. No Hezbollah confirmation. Senior Israeli official tells Haaretz: 'There is no ceasefire in Lebanon.' IDF to continue operations to Litani River.
verificationNote: Axios, PBS, Bloomberg, NBC News, Haaretz confirmed ceasefire announcement and IDF non-withdrawal.

text: BLOCKADE GOES GLOBAL: Gen. Caine (CJCS) announces US Navy will now intercept any Iranian-flagged vessel ANYWHERE IN THE WORLD — including Pacific AOR under Adm. Paparo. 13 ships turned back in 3 days. No boardings yet. Contraband includes weapons, nuclear materials, crude/refined oil, iron, steel, aluminum. Hegseth: US 'locked and loaded' to attack Iran's electrical power grid if talks fail. 'Reloading with more power than ever before.'
verificationNote: Breaking Defense, Stars and Stripes, Military.com, Bloomberg confirmed global blockade expansion and 13 ships.

text: QASMIYEH BRIDGE DESTROYED: Israeli warplanes hit the last remaining bridge over the Litani River with 2 consecutive strikes. ~10% of Lebanon's territory now fully severed from the rest of the country. Lebanese army evacuated checkpoint. Separately, 4 paramedics killed in Mayfadoun triple-tap — 3 ambulance teams hit in succession. 91+ Lebanese medical workers killed since March 2. Ceasefire announced hours later — pattern of escalation before de-escalation.
verificationNote: Xinhua, CBC News, Naharnet confirmed bridge destruction. Al Jazeera, Newsweek confirmed paramedic killings.

text: IRAN THREATS: Mohsen Rezaei warns US warships in Strait of Hormuz are 'under our missile launchers' and threatens to 'sink them all.' Claims launchers aimed at USS Abraham Lincoln. Meanwhile Pakistan Army Chief Munir meets Iranian Parliament Speaker — Pakistani sources say visit 'significantly contributed to narrowing differences.' White House 'optimistic' about deal. Round 2 of US-Iran talks likely in Islamabad next week. Ceasefire expires April 22.
verificationNote: Press TV confirmed Rezaei statement. Bloomberg, Al Jazeera confirmed Pakistan mediation progress.

text: Day 48 EVE roundup: CEASEFIRE — Trump announces 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire (5 PM ET). Israel won't withdraw from south Lebanon. No Hezbollah confirmation. BLOCKADE GLOBAL — US Navy to intercept Iranian ships worldwide. 13 ships turned back. Hegseth threatens Iran's electrical grid. INFRASTRUCTURE — Qasmiyeh Bridge destroyed, last Litani crossing. 4 paramedics killed Mayfadoun. IRAN — Rezaei threatens to sink US warships. Pakistan 'narrowing differences.' MARKETS — S&P pulls back 0.3% from record. Oil: Brent $94.89 WTI $91.29. Gas: $4.093. Sanctions waiver expires April 19.
verificationNote: Compiled from Axios, PBS, Bloomberg, Breaking Defense, Xinhua, CBC, Newsweek, Press TV, Al Jazeera, AAA.

text: LEBANON CEASEFIRE NOW IN EFFECT — midnight Beirut time. But last hours were BRUTAL: 8 killed in Nabatieh AFTER Trump announcement. Hezbollah fired 10 rockets 2 hours before ceasefire — 3 Israelis wounded, 2 severely. IDF intercepted 8. Israel will NOT withdraw from south Lebanon. No Hezbollah confirmation they will honor it. First direct Israel-Lebanon diplomatic engagement since 1983.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, PBS, Haaretz, Middle East Monitor confirmed all details.

text: WAR POWERS FAILS AGAIN: House vote 214-213 — third failed attempt. Only Rep. Jared Golden (D-ME) crossed party lines. War Powers Act 60-day deadline approaching end of April. Meanwhile Democrats file 6 impeachment articles against Hegseth: unauthorized war, civilian casualties (Minab girls school), classified info. Dead on arrival but establishes congressional record.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Axios, NBC News confirmed war powers vote and impeachment filing.

text: IRAN ESCALATION THREAT: IRGC Maj. Gen. Abdollahi threatens to block ALL shipping — Gulf, Sea of Oman, AND Red Sea. Houthis echo threat on Bab al-Mandeb. USS George H.W. Bush CSG routing around entire Africa to avoid Houthi missile zone. Iran suspended ALL petrochemical exports since April 13. Losing $400M/day from blockade. Treasury sanctions 24+ entities in Shamkhani oil network. Sanctions waiver expires April 19.
verificationNote: Fox News, NBC News confirmed IRGC threat. CBS confirmed Houthi threat. US Treasury confirmed sanctions.

text: MARKET WATCH: S&P 500 above 7,000 for FIRST TIME. Nasdaq 10-day win streak (longest since 2009). Brent 5.86 (+20.1% from pre-war). WTI 1.62 (+21.3%). US crude inventories drop 9.13M bbl (vs +154K expected) — biggest draw in months. EIA forecasts Brent Q2 peak 15/bbl. Gas .093 (flat). Israeli shekel 30-year high 2.9954/USD. Iran rial all-time low 1.75M/USD. IMF cuts global growth to 3.1%.
verificationNote: CNBC, PBS, EIA, Trading Economics, IMF confirmed all data points.

text: Day 49 roundup: CEASEFIRE — Israel-Lebanon ceasefire in effect since midnight Beirut time. 8 killed after announcement. Hezbollah 10 rockets before ceasefire (3 wounded). CONGRESS — War powers fails 214-213. Hegseth impeachment filed. IRAN — IRGC threatens ALL shipping. Pakistan army chief in Tehran. No date for Islamabad Round 2. SANCTIONS — Treasury Economic Fury 24+ entities. Waiver expires April 19. MARKETS — S&P 7,000 record. Brent 5.86 WTI 1.62. Gas .093. Iran rial all-time low.
verificationNote: Compiled from Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Middle East Monitor, PBS, Axios, Fox News, CNBC, US Treasury, IMF.

text: CEASEFIRE VIOLATIONS: Lebanese army confirms Israeli artillery shelling of Khiam and Dibbine approximately 30 minutes after midnight ceasefire took effect. Machinegun fire during sweeping operations. Reconnaissance aircraft over Rashaya and Bekaa Valley. Army officially accused Israel of violations.
verificationNote: ANI News, Tribune India, RTE confirmed Lebanese army reports.

text: BLOCKADE UPDATE: Ship spoofing detected — Iran-linked tanker 'Alicia' transmitted false AIS data attempting to breach US naval blockade. 10,000+ US troops and 12+ warships enforcing. USS George H.W. Bush CSG en route as third carrier (currently off Namibia). 13-14 ships turned back total.
verificationNote: ABC News, Military.com, Stars and Stripes confirmed.

text: HORMUZ SUMMIT: France and UK co-hosting 40-nation videoconference today to plan defensive multinational naval mission for Strait of Hormuz. Both explicitly reject joining US blockade. UK announces military planning meeting next week. 20,000+ seafarers stranded.
verificationNote: Al-Monitor, Times of Israel, Tribune India confirmed.

text: NUCLEAR TALKS: Pakistan hints at 'major breakthrough' on Iran nuclear issue. Army chief Munir met Araghchi, Ghalibaf, IRGC commander in Tehran. Three sticking points remain: nuclear freeze duration (5-20 years), fate of 440kg HEU, Hormuz control. Trump says talks could resume 'this weekend.'
verificationNote: Bloomberg, Al Jazeera confirmed Pakistan mediation and nuclear breakthrough hints.

text: OIL SURGE: Brent jumps to $98.25 (+23.1%), WTI $93.47 (+23.8%) — surging 4% as doubts resurface over US-Iran negotiations. IEA April report: March saw 10.1M bpd supply lost — 'largest disruption in history of global oil market.' EIA raised 2026 Brent forecast to $96/bbl.
verificationNote: CNBC, IEA, Rigzone, OilPriceAPI confirmed.

text: Day 49 PM roundup: CEASEFIRE VIOLATIONS — Israeli shelling Khiam/Dibbine 30 min after midnight. Lebanese army accuses Israel. HORMUZ — France-UK 40-nation summit plans defensive mission. ANTALYA — Pak/Saudi/Turkey/Egypt quad pushes Islamabad Round 2. NUCLEAR — Pakistan hints 'major breakthrough.' BLOCKADE — Ship spoofing detected, 13-14 ships turned back. text: OIL ALERT: Brent crude surges past $99 to $99.39/bbl (+24.5% from pre-war) — highest since early March when Hormuz first closed. WTI $93.74 (+24.1%). Markets pricing in growing doubt that US-Iran negotiations will produce a deal before April 22 ceasefire expiration. EIA raised 2026 Brent forecast to $96/bbl average.
verificationNote: Xinhua, Rigzone, Trading Economics confirmed.

text: IRAN REJECTS DEAL: Deputy FM Khatibzadeh at Antalya Diplomacy Forum says Iran will not accept any temporary ceasefire — demands comprehensive end to ALL hostilities 'from Lebanon to the Red Sea.' This is a hardening of Iran's negotiating position with only 5 days until April 22 ceasefire expiration. Meanwhile Pakistan, Saudi, Turkey, Egypt FMs meeting at Antalya to push for Islamabad Round 2.
verificationNote: Anadolu Agency, ANI News, TRT World confirmed.

text: HORMUZ SUMMIT: Macron-Starmer 40-nation summit concluded with plans for defensive multinational naval mission to secure Strait when conditions permit. Military planning meeting next week. Key: both France and UK explicitly REJECT joining US blockade. 20,000+ seafarers stranded. Merz and Meloni attended in person. US notably NOT invited.
verificationNote: AP/ClickOrlando, NBC News, Insurance Journal, Al Jazeera confirmed.

text: UN OHCHR experts condemn Israel's 'unprecedented bombing in Lebanon after ceasefire announcement.' Lebanese army continues reporting violations — artillery in Khiam/Dibbine, reconnaissance over Rashaya/Bekaa. Separately, Araghchi posted on X: 'The U.S. must choose — ceasefire or continued war via Israel. It cannot have both.'
verificationNote: UN OHCHR press release, Arab News, NPR confirmed.

text: Day 49 Late roundup: Brent surges past $99 to $99.39 (+24.5%), WTI $93.74 (+24.1%) — highest since early March. Iran rejects temporary ceasefire at Antalya — demands end to ALL hostilities. UN OHCHR condemns Israel's post-ceasefire bombing. Hormuz summit: 40 nations plan defensive naval mission, UK/France reject US blockade. Araghchi: 'US must choose ceasefire or war via Israel.'
